static void bfq_set_budget_timeout(struct bfq_data *bfqd,
				   struct bfq_queue *bfqq)
{
	unsigned int timeout_coeff;

	if (bfqq->wr_cur_max_time == bfqd->bfq_wr_rt_max_time)
		timeout_coeff = 1;
	else
		timeout_coeff = bfqq->entity.weight / bfqq->entity.orig_weight;

	bfqd->last_budget_start = blk_time_get();

	bfqq->budget_timeout = jiffies +
		bfqd->bfq_timeout * timeout_coeff;
}

/*
 * Budget timeout is not implemented through a dedicated timer, but
 * just checked on request arrivals and completions, as well as on
 * idle timer expirations.
 */
static bool bfq_bfqq_budget_timeout(struct bfq_queue *bfqq)
{
	return time_is_before_eq_jiffies(bfqq->budget_timeout);
}

/*
 * If we expire a queue that is actively waiting (i.e., with the
 * device idled) for the arrival of a new request, then we may incur
 * the timestamp misalignment problem described in the body of the
 * function __bfq_activate_entity. Hence we return true only if this
 * condition does not hold, or if the queue is slow enough to deserve
 * only to be kicked off for preserving a high throughput.
 */
static bool bfq_may_expire_for_budg_timeout(struct bfq_queue *bfqq)
{
	bfq_log_bfqq(bfqq->bfqd, bfqq,
		"may_budget_timeout: wait_request %d left %d timeout %d",
		bfq_bfqq_wait_request(bfqq),
			bfq_bfqq_budget_left(bfqq) >=  bfqq->entity.budget / 3,
		bfq_bfqq_budget_timeout(bfqq));

	return (!bfq_bfqq_wait_request(bfqq) ||
		bfq_bfqq_budget_left(bfqq) >=  bfqq->entity.budget / 3)
		&&
		bfq_bfqq_budget_timeout(bfqq);
}

BFQ’s default budget timeout is `HZ / 8` jiffies: `const int bfq_timeout = HZ / 8` in [block/bfq-iosched.c](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-muthuishere-muthu-gitworkspace-nexus-workspace-brain/dc4417dd-0040-4de7-82a4-10f10843dc78/scratchpad/proof/linux/block/bfq-iosched.c:203). Per queue, `bfq_set_budget_timeout()` sets `bfqq->budget_timeout = jiffies + bfqd->bfq_timeout * timeout_coeff` in [block/bfq-iosched.c](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-muthuishere-muthu-gitworkspace-nexus-workspace-brain/dc4417dd-0040-4de7-82a4-10f10843dc78/scratchpad/proof/linux/block/bfq-iosched.c:3285).

The direct expiry check is `bfq_bfqq_budget_timeout()`, which returns `time_is_before_eq_jiffies(bfqq->budget_timeout)` in [block/bfq-iosched.c](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-muthuishere-muthu-gitworkspace-nexus-workspace-brain/dc4417dd-0040-4de7-82a4-10f10843dc78/scratchpad/proof/linux/block/bfq-iosched.c:4460). The practical gate is `bfq_may_expire_for_budg_timeout()` in [block/bfq-iosched.c](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-muthuishere-muthu-gitworkspace-nexus-workspace-brain/dc4417dd-0040-4de7-82a4-10f10843dc78/scratchpad/proof/linux/block/bfq-iosched.c:4473).

When it fires, `bfq_select_queue()` expires the current in-service queue with `bfq_bfqq_expire(..., BFQQE_BUDGET_TIMEOUT)` in [block/bfq-iosched.c](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-muthuishere-muthu-gitworkspace-nexus-workspace-brain/dc4417dd-0040-4de7-82a4-10f10843dc78/scratchpad/proof/linux/block/bfq-iosched.c:4818) and [block/bfq-iosched.c](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-muthuishere-muthu-gitworkspace-nexus-workspace-brain/dc4417dd-0040-4de7-82a4-10f10843dc78/scratchpad/proof/linux/block/bfq-iosched.c:5017). Expiration deletes the queue from busy if empty, otherwise requeues it, then resets service state via `__bfq_bfqd_reset_in_service()` in [block/bfq-iosched.c](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-muthuishere-muthu-gitworkspace-nexus-workspace-brain/dc4417dd-0040-4de7-82a4-10f10843dc78/scratchpad/proof/linux/block/bfq-iosched.c:3915). That reset clears wait-request, cancels the idle timer, sets `bfqd->in_service_queue = NULL`, and clears `in_service_entity` up the hierarchy in [block/bfq-wf2q.c](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-muthuishere-muthu-gitworkspace-nexus-workspace-brain/dc4417dd-0040-4de7-82a4-10f10843dc78/scratchpad/proof/linux/block/bfq-wf2q.c:1547).
